A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:Village de vacances]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|fr|Village de vacances}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Holiday_village "Talk:Holiday village"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation"). |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(abbreviated **HV**) is a holiday [resort](/wiki/Resort "Resort") where the visitors stay in [villas](/wiki/Villa "Villa"). There is a central area with [shops](/wiki/Retailing#Shops_and_stores "Retailing"), [entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment"), and other [amenities](/wiki/Amenity "Amenity").[[1]](#cite_note-1) One example is [Center Parcs](/wiki/Center_Parcs_Europe "Center Parcs Europe").
Stanisław Pietkiewicz, Stefan Żmuda: *Słownik pojęć geograficznych*. Warsaw: Wiedza Powszechna, 1973, p. 238. (in Polish)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Settlement mostly consisting of holiday homes

This article is about a holiday village in general. For the First Choice Holidays Holiday Village brand, see [First Choice (UK)](/wiki/First_Choice_(UK) "First Choice (UK)").

"Tourist village" redirects here. For other uses, see [Tourist village (Indonesia)](/wiki/Tourist_village_(Indonesia) "Tourist village (Indonesia)").

A **holiday village** (abbreviated **HV**) is a holiday [resort](/wiki/Resort "Resort") where the visitors stay in [villas](/wiki/Villa "Villa"). There is a central area with [shops](/wiki/Retailing#Shops_and_stores "Retailing"), [entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment"), and other [amenities](/wiki/Amenity "Amenity").[[1]](#cite_note-1) One example is [Center Parcs](/wiki/Center_Parcs_Europe "Center Parcs Europe").
